sections.heading: Cluster Limits
sections.body:
| Component                                                   | Limit                                                                                                           |
| ----------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Shards in Multi-Region Clusters                             | 12                                                                                                              |
| Shards in Single-region clusters                            | No hard limit, but contact MongoDB Support for more than 80 shards.                                             |
| Cross-region network permissions for a Multi-Region Cluster | 40If any project's cluster spans more than 40 regions, you can't create a multi-region cluster in this project. |
| Electable Nodes per Replica Set or Shard                    | 7                                                                                                               |
| Config Server Tier (minimum and maximum)                    | M30                                                                                                             |

sections.heading: Connection Limits and Cluster Tier
sections.body:
Atlas sets the [limits for concurrent incoming connections](manual/reference/configuration-options) based on the cluster tier and
[class](storage-class-ui). Atlas connection limits apply per node. For
[sharded clusters](manual/sharding), Atlas connection limits apply per
[mongos](manual/core/sharded-cluster-query-router) router. The number of
[mongos](manual/core/sharded-cluster-query-router) routers is equal to
the number of replica set nodes across all shards.

Your [read preference](manual/core/read-preference) also contributes to the
total number of connections Atlas can allocate for a given query.

> **Example:** Your `M10` cluster has three nodes with a 1500 connection limit per node.
> Atlas reserves 10 connections per node. If you set your [read preference](manual/core/read-preference) to [secondary](manual/core/read-preference), Atlas can read from
> the two secondary nodes for a combined 2980 connection limit.
>
> The cluster connection limits are enforced for AWS only when connecting over
> private link for clusters on the supported versions:
>
> - 8.1 and v8.1.0+
>
> - 8.0 and v8.0.10+
>
> - 7.0 and v7.0.22+

sections.heading: Increase Connection Limit
sections.body:
> **Note:** Atlas scales on memory and compute use, not on connections. However, increased active connections may increase load, which may create need to scale your cluster if other solutions are not viable.

If you are nearing or have reached the maximum number of connections your Atlas cluster can handle, you have several options available to redistribute connections or increase your limit:

- [Tune your application](manual/administration/performance-tuning) to optimize connection usage.

- [Enable connection pooling](manual/administration/connection-pool-overview) in your application.

- [Cluster Auto-Scaling](cluster-autoscaling) to automatically scale your application as needed. This option is particularly helpful if your application dynamically creates additional connections. An elastic database worker pool would be an example of such an application.

- Permanently scale your [Modify the Cluster Tier](scale-cluster-instance).

AWS

General Class

| Cluster Tier | Maximum Connections Per Node |
| ------------ | ---------------------------- |
| M10          | 1500                         |
| M20          | 3000                         |
| M30          | 3000                         |
| M40          | 6000                         |
| M50          | 16000                        |
| M60          | 32000                        |
| M80          | 96000                        |
| M140         | 96000                        |
| M200         | 128000                       |
| M300         | 128000                       |

Low-CPU Class

| Cluster Tier | Maximum Connections Per Node |
| ------------ | ---------------------------- |
| M40          | 4000                         |
| M50          | 16000                        |
| M60          | 32000                        |
| M80          | 64000                        |
| M140         | 96000                        |
| M200         | 128000                       |
| M300         | 128000                       |
| M400         | 128000                       |
| M700         | 128000                       |

Azure and GCP

General Class

| Cluster Tier | Maximum Connections Per Node |
| ------------ | ---------------------------- |
| M10          | 1500                         |
| M20          | 3000                         |
| M30          | 3000                         |
| M40          | 6000                         |
| M50          | 16000                        |
| M60          | 32000                        |
| M80          | 64000                        |
| M140         | 96000                        |
| M200         | 128000                       |
| M300         | 128000                       |
| M400         | 128000                       |
| M600         | 128000                       |

Low-CPU Class

| Cluster Tier | Maximum Connections Per Node |
| ------------ | ---------------------------- |
| M40          | 6000                         |
| M50          | 16000                        |
| M60          | 32000                        |
| M80          | 64000                        |
| M200         | 128000                       |
| M300         | 128000                       |
| M400         | 128000                       |

> **Note:** Atlas reserves a small number of connections to each Atlas
> cluster for supporting Atlas services. To learn more about
> Atlas reserved connections, contact Atlas support.

sections.heading: Connection Rate Limits
sections.body:
Atlas applies connection rate limits to protect cluster availability and
ensure optimal performance. These limits restrict the number of new connections
that each node in the cluster processes per second.

| Cluster Tier | Maximum New Connections Per Second Per Node |
| ------------ | ------------------------------------------- |
| M10          | 15                                          |
| M20          | 15                                          |

> **Note:** Connection rate limits apply only to `M10` and `M20` cluster tiers. Other cluster
> tiers don't have connection rate limits. Connection rate limits don't include
> connections from Atlas internal services.
>
> If the connection rate limit is exceeded, new connection attempts are queued,
> and if the period of overload is sustained, some connections are dropped.
> This feature helps maintain cluster availability by preventing connection
> overload on these cluster tiers.

sections.heading: Collection and Index Limits
sections.body:
While there is no hard limit on the number of collections in a single
cluster, the performance of a cluster might degrade if it serves a large
number of collections and indexes. Larger collections have a greater
impact on performance.

The recommended maximum combined number of collections and indexes by
Atlas cluster tier are as follows:

| Cluster tier | Recommended maximum             |
| ------------ | ------------------------------- |
| M10          | 5,000 collections and indexes   |
| M20 / M30    | 10,000 collections and indexes  |
| M40+         | 100,000 collections and indexes |

If you exceed the recommended maximum number of collections and indexes, database
operations might run more slowly. [Checkpoints](manual/core/wiredtiger)
take longer to complete, which reduces operation speed. In some cases, operations
can stall if your usage exceeds your system's `ulimit` settings, which set
limitations on the number of system resources that you can use.

> **Tip:** To learn more about `ulimit` settings, see [UNIX ulimit Settings for Self-Managed Deployments](manual/reference/ulimit) in the MongoDB Server Manual.

The performance impact of exceeding the recommended maximum resource number
depends your collection usage. Performance impacts are more likely if you use
the majority of the existing collections consistently.

sections.heading: Increase Collection and Index Limits
sections.body:
If you need to increase the number of collections or indexes in your Atlas
cluster, consider upgrading your [Modify the Cluster Tier](scale-cluster-instance).

sections.heading: Organization and Project Limits
sections.body:
| Component                                                                    | Limit                                                                                                                                                               |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Database Users per Atlas Project                                             | 100                                                                                                                                                                 |
| Atlas Users per Atlas Project                                                | 500                                                                                                                                                                 |
| Atlas Users per Atlas Organization                                           | 500                                                                                                                                                                 |
| API Keys per Atlas Organization                                              | 500                                                                                                                                                                 |
| Access List Entries per Atlas Project                                        | 200                                                                                                                                                                 |
| Users per Atlas Team                                                         | 250                                                                                                                                                                 |
| Teams per Atlas Project                                                      | 100                                                                                                                                                                 |
| Teams per Atlas Organization                                                 | 250                                                                                                                                                                 |
| Teams per Atlas User                                                         | 100                                                                                                                                                                 |
| Organizations per Atlas User                                                 | 250                                                                                                                                                                 |
| Linked Organizations per Atlas Paying Organization                           | 250                                                                                                                                                                 |
| Clusters per Atlas Project                                                   | 25                                                                                                                                                                  |
| Projects per Atlas User                                                      | 250                                                                                                                                                                 |
| Projects per Atlas Organization                                              | 250                                                                                                                                                                 |
| Custom MongoDB roles per Atlas Project                                       | 100                                                                                                                                                                 |
| Assigned Roles per Database User                                             | 100                                                                                                                                                                 |
| Federated database instances per Atlas Project                               | 25                                                                                                                                                                  |
| Total Network Peering Connections per Atlas Project                          | 50Atlas limits the number of nodes per Network Peering connection based on the CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block and the region selected for the project. |
| Pending Network Peering Connections per Atlas Project                        | 25                                                                                                                                                                  |
| AWS PrivateLink Addressable Target Nodes per Region                          | 50                                                                                                                                                                  |
| Azure Private Link Addressable Target Nodes per Region                       | 150                                                                                                                                                                 |
| Google Cloud GCP Private Service Connect Addressable Target Nodes per Region | 50                                                                                                                                                                  |
| Global Cluster shard keys per Atlas Project                                  | 40                                                                                                                                                                  |

> **Note:** **Addressable Target Nodes** refer to the maximum number of MongoDB nodes
> that can be accessed through private endpoints per project, per region.
> Target nodes include mongod instances in replica sets, mongos instances
> in sharded clusters, and BI Connector for Atlas instances across all dedicated clusters in
> the project.

star You can deploy only one Free cluster (formerly known as `M0`) per
project. However, if you have sales contracts with MongoDB, you might
be able to deploy more Free clusters per project. To learn more,
contact a MongoDB sales representative.

sections.heading: Label Limits
sections.body:
Atlas limits the length of particular labels for components.

| Component                                               | Character Limit | RegEx Pattern                                                  |
| ------------------------------------------------------- | --------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Cluster Name                                            | 64 1            | ^(\[a-zA-Z0-9]\(\[a-zA-Z0-9-])\{0,21}(?\<!-)(\[\w]\{0,42}))$ 2 |
| Project Name                                            | 64              | ^\[\p\{L}\p\{N}\\-\_.(),:&@+']\{1,64}$ 3                       |
| Organization Name                                       | 64              | ^\[\p\{L}\p\{N}\\-\_.(),:&@+']\{1,64}$ 3                       |
| API (Application Programming Interface) Key Description | 250             |                                                                |

[^1]: If you have [FAQ on changes to Azure network peering](atlas-faq-azure-gcp-peering-only), the cluster name
    character limit is 23.

[^2]: Atlas uses the first *23 characters* of a cluster name.
    These characters must be unique within the containing project.
    Cluster names with fewer than 23 characters can't end with a hyphen
    (`-`). Cluster names with more than 23 characters can't have a
    hyphen as the 23rd character.

[^3]: Organization and Project names can include any Unicode letter or
    number plus the following punctuation: `-_.(),:&@+'`.

sections.heading: Database User Privilege Limits
sections.body:
To learn more about unsupported commands, see the unsupported commands
for [limits or are unsupported](free-tier-command-limitations) and
[replSetResizeOplog](paid-tier-command-limitations).

sections.heading: Multi-Cloud Deployment Limits
sections.body:
Multi-cloud deployments have different limits. To learn more,
see [Connections to Multi-Cloud Deployments](multi-cloud-limitation).
